descriptor: Largest US steelmaker and recycler, EAF-based. HQ Charlotte, NC. NYSE: NUE.
snapshot: Sheet, plate, structural, bar, DRI, scrap
Construction, data centers, renewables, energy, auto

assets.note: 300+ operating facilities per 2026 company filings. Major production assets below; bar mill capacity not re-verified.

sites.name: Sheet mills (6, incl 51% CSI)
sites.loc: SC, AR, IN, AL, KY, CA

sites.name: Plate mills (3)
sites.loc: Hertford NC, Tuscaloosa AL, Brandenburg KY

sites.name: Downstream units
sites.type: Joists, deck, buildings, doors (Rytec), data-center racks

suppliers: **Samarco** (BHP/Vale JV, Brazil): DRI imports to Louisiana, 5-year extended agreement
**Ovintiv** (ex-Encana): legacy gas position incl. 49% of ~54,000 acres, South Piceance Basin
**NextEra Energy Resources**: solar agreement backing Kentucky operations
**DJJ** (in-house): ~5 Mt/yr processing + pig iron/HBI brokerage from thousands of US dealers
jvs: **Yamato Kogyo** (Japan): 49% of Nucor-Yamato structural mill, Blytheville AR
**JFE Steel** (Japan): 49% of California Steel Industries; 50/50 Mexico auto-galv JV
customers: Sheet: ~85% contract customers in 2025 (10-K); names not disclosed, no customer >10% of revenue
Plate/structural/rebar/SBQ: nearly all spot via service centers (likely) incl. Ryerson, Olympic Steel
Data centers: claims ~95% of a build's steel; sees 60M sq ft of DC construction in 2026, +30% y/y
Utilities/telecom: Towers & Structures unit; 3rd plant coming in Brigham City UT ($200M)

angles: Metal spread ~$660/st (HRC $1,130 vs busheling ~$470): EAF margins near records, scrap sellers have room to push
Nucor held CSP flat 2 straight weeks after 23-week, +$380 rally: watch for the top, hedge forward sales
Prime scrap is their pain point: busheling tightness and pig iron/DRI alternatives open the DJJ conversation
WV 3Mt sheet ramp 2027: Ohio Valley HRC basis pressure coming, position accordingly
Q2 guided higher on price, lead times elevated: consider CME HRC hedges before restock season
$2.5B capex plus stated M&A appetite: downstream bolt-ons (doors, racks, towers), not commodity assets
Auto and resi construction lagging: Berkeley galv line needs auto demand that hasn't shown up yet

developments.event: Held HR coil CSP at $1,130/st for second straight week
232 carve-outs widened; COO Sumoski retired Jun 13
Q1 beat: EPS $3.23 vs $2.70-2.80 guide, record mill shipments
Section 232 expanded Apr 6: 50% on full customs value, more derivatives
Laxton became President and COO; Topalian eyeing M&A
Capex ~$2.5B; Berkeley galv line mid-2026; WV mill commissioning late 2026

tariffs: 232 steel at 50% since Jun 2025; Apr 2026 action: full customs value, wider derivative list
Net beneficiary: ~100% US melt; only import exposure is untariffed Brazilian ore/DRI
50% duty prices out foreign HRC, underpinning $1,100+ domestic prices
Jun 2026 carve-outs (15% equipment, 10% high-US-content capital goods): marginal pricing-umbrella erosion
Risk: tariff-inflated steel costs push derivative-product customers to cut volume or import finished goods
financials: Q1'26: sales $9.50B (+21% y/y), net income $743M, EPS $3.23, EBITDA $1.51B
Record steel mills segment shipments in Q1'26
Q2'26 guided higher: stable mill volumes, better realized pricing across segments
Capex ~$2.5B for 2026, internally funded; investment-grade credit
Stock +23% in 3 months; no red flags in current filings
market: HRC: Nucor CSP $1,130/st (Jul 6), SMU $1,160/st (Jun 30); +$380 over 23 weeks, now plateaued
Busheling/prompt scrap: $465-471/t all year (RMDAS); July settled sideways
Spread read: mills captured the entire rally; scrap has catch-up potential if pig iron/DRI tightens
Sheet and plate lead times elevated (SMU, Jun 25); CR/coated flat
Global: China rebar near 8-month lows (~CNY 3,060/t), EU import curbs; US decoupled via 232
